sections.paragraph:
γενεά, ᾶς, ἡ [γίνομαι; prim. a kinship term ‘people (group), tribe, family’]

—1. ‘persons with common interests’, an extension of the kinship idea kind of people Lk 16:8.

—2. ‘people linked as contemporaries’, generation Mt 11:16; Mk 13:30; Lk 7:31; Phil 2:15; Hb 3:10 al.

—3. ‘a span of time loosely equal to a generation’, freq. of succession of generations Mt 1:17; Lk 1:48; Ac 14:16; 15:21; Eph 3:21; Col 1:26.

—4. ‘family line’, posterity, w. focus on the progenitor Ac 8:33, the point here being that the sufferer dies before beginning a family (a typical motif in ancient lamentation).
